members.name: AddDevice
members.summary: Adds the given [UPNPDevice] to the list of discovered devices.

members.name: AddPortMapping
members.signature: public int AddPortMapping(int port, int portInternal, String desc, String proto, int duration)
members.summary: Adds a mapping to forward the external [param port] (between 1 and 65535, although recommended to use port 1024 or above) on the default gateway (see [method get_gateway]) to the [param port_internal] on the local machine for the given protocol [param proto] (either [code]"TCP"[/code] or [code]"UDP"[/code], with UDP being the default). If a port mapping for the given port and protocol combination already exists on that gateway device, this method tries to overwrite it. If that is not desired, you can retrieve the gateway manually with [method get_gateway] and call [method add_port_mapping] on it, if any. Note that forwarding a well-known port (below 1024) with UPnP may fail depending on the device. Depending on the gateway device, if a mapping for that port already exists, it will either be updated or it will refuse this command due to that conflict, especially if the existing mapping for that port wasn't created via UPnP or points to a different network address (or device) than this one. If [param port_internal] is [code]0[/code] (the default), the same port number is used for both the external and the internal port (the [param port] value). The description ([param desc]) is shown in some routers management UIs and can be used to point out which application added the mapping. The mapping's lease [param duration] can be limited by specifying a duration in seconds. The default of [code]0[/code] means no duration, i.e. a permanent lease and notably some devices only support these permanent leases. Note that whether permanent or not, this is only a request and the gateway may still decide at any point to remove the mapping (which usually happens on a reboot of the gateway, when its external IP address changes, or on some models when it detects a port mapping has become inactive, i.e. had no traffic for multiple minutes). If not [code]0[/code] (permanent), the allowed range according to spec is between [code]120[/code] (2 minutes) and [code]86400[/code] seconds (24 hours). See [enum UPNPResult] for possible return values.

members.name: ClearDevices
members.summary: Clears the list of discovered devices.

members.name: DeletePortMapping
members.signature: public int DeletePortMapping(int port, String proto)
members.summary: Deletes the port mapping for the given port and protocol combination on the default gateway (see [method get_gateway]) if one exists. [param port] must be a valid port between 1 and 65535, [param proto] can be either [code]"TCP"[/code] or [code]"UDP"[/code]. May be refused for mappings pointing to addresses other than this one, for well-known ports (below 1024), or for mappings not added via UPnP. See [enum UPNPResult] for possible return values.

members.name: Discover
members.signature: public int Discover(int timeout, int ttl, String deviceFilter)
members.summary: Discovers local [UPNPDevice]s. Clears the list of previously discovered devices. Filters for IGD (InternetGatewayDevice) type devices by default, as those manage port forwarding. [param timeout] is the time to wait for responses in milliseconds. [param ttl] is the time-to-live; only touch this if you know what you're doing. See [enum UPNPResult] for possible return values.

members.name: GetDevice
members.summary: Returns the [UPNPDevice] at the given [param index].

members.name: GetGateway
members.summary: Returns the default gateway. That is the first discovered [UPNPDevice] that is also a valid IGD (InternetGatewayDevice).

members.name: QueryExternalAddress
members.summary: Returns the external [IP] address of the default gateway (see [method get_gateway]) as string. Returns an empty string on error.

members.name: RemoveDevice
members.summary: Removes the device at [param index] from the list of discovered devices.

members.name: SetDevice
members.signature: public void SetDevice(int index, UPNPDevice device)
members.summary: Sets the device at [param index] from the list of discovered devices to [param device].

members.name: DiscoverIpv6
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], IPv6 is used for [UPNPDevice] discovery.

members.name: DiscoverLocalPort
members.summary: If [code]0[/code], the local port to use for discovery is chosen automatically by the system. If [code]1[/code], discovery will be done from the source port 1900 (same as destination port). Otherwise, the value will be used as the port.

members.name: DiscoverMulticastIf
members.summary: Multicast interface to use for discovery. Uses the default multicast interface if empty.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_ACTION_FAILED
values.summary: The action failed.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_CONFLICT_WITH_OTHER_MAPPING
values.summary: Conflict with an existing port mapping.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_CONFLICT_WITH_OTHER_MECHANISM
values.summary: Conflict with other mechanism. May be returned instead of [constant UPNP_RESULT_CONFLICT_WITH_OTHER_MAPPING] if a port mapping conflicts with an existing one.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_EXT_PORT_MUST_BE_WILDCARD
values.summary: The external port value must be a wildcard.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_EXT_PORT_WILDCARD_NOT_PERMITTED
values.summary: The [UPNPDevice] does not allow wildcard values for the external port.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_HTTP_ERROR
values.summary: HTTP error.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INCONSISTENT_PARAMETERS
values.summary: Inconsistent parameters.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INT_PORT_WILDCARD_NOT_PERMITTED
values.summary: The [UPNPDevice] does not allow wildcard values for the internal port.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_ARGS
values.summary: Invalid arguments.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_DURATION
values.summary: Invalid duration.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_GATEWAY
values.summary: Invalid gateway.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_PARAM
values.summary: Invalid parameter.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_PORT
values.summary: Invalid port.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_PROTOCOL
values.summary: Invalid protocol.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_INVALID_RESPONSE
values.summary: Invalid response.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_MEM_ALLOC_ERROR
values.summary: Error allocating memory.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_NOT_AUTHORIZED
values.summary: Not authorized to use the command on the [UPNPDevice]. May be returned when the user disabled UPNP on their router.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_NO_DEVICES
values.summary: No devices available. You may need to call [method discover] first, or discovery didn't detect any valid [UPNPDevice]s.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_NO_GATEWAY
values.summary: No gateway available. You may need to call [method discover] first, or discovery didn't detect any valid IGDs (InternetGatewayDevices).

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_NO_PORT_MAPS_AVAILABLE
values.summary: No port maps are available. May also be returned if port mapping functionality is not available.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_NO_SUCH_ENTRY_IN_ARRAY
values.summary: No such entry in array. May be returned if a given port, protocol combination is not found on a [UPNPDevice].

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_ONLY_PERMANENT_LEASE_SUPPORTED
values.summary: Only permanent leases are supported. Do not use the [code]duration[/code] parameter when adding port mappings.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_PORT_MAPPING_NOT_FOUND
values.summary: No port mapping was found for the given port, protocol combination on the given [UPNPDevice].

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_REMOTE_HOST_MUST_BE_WILDCARD
values.summary: The remote host value must be a wildcard.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_SAME_PORT_VALUES_REQUIRED
values.summary: External and internal port values must be the same.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_SOCKET_ERROR
values.summary: Socket error.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_SRC_IP_WILDCARD_NOT_PERMITTED
values.summary: The [UPNPDevice] does not allow wildcard values for the source IP address.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_SUCCESS
values.summary: UPNP command or discovery was successful.

values.name: UPNP_RESULT_UNKNOWN_ERROR
values.summary: Unknown error.

constants.summary: UPNP command or discovery was successful.
